name: Vacuum-energy sequestering
summary: Couples the QFT vacuum energy to a global modulus that enforces cancellation against the historic average of trace contributions over the cosmological history. Separates 'particle physics' vacuum energy from 'gravitating' vacuum energy without demanding fine-tuning of the renormalized Lambda.

addresses_puzzles.note: Removes the radiative-correction component of the cosmological-constant problem; does not explain why the residual integration constant is at meV^4. Subtle predictions for the cosmological history distinguish it from LambdaCDM in principle.

new_symmetries: a global rescaling acting on the metric and Planck mass
introduces.note: Coupling and effective UV cutoff for the trace integration; the framework is engineered to be insensitive to QFT vacuum-energy radiative corrections.

history: Kaloper and Padilla (2014) introduced the local sequestering construction. Manifestly local 'monodromy' versions (2016) addressed objections. Distinct from older vacuum-energy cancellation mechanisms (e.g., Tseytlin) by its use of cosmological-history averaging.

references.citation: Kaloper and Padilla, 'Sequestering the standard model vacuum energy', PRL 112, 091304 (2014)
